angle: Bullseye HR Coordinator. Maps directly to Jamie's real HR-coordination work: Kronos HR intern (HRIS/recruitment/comp, job postings, drafting JDs, campus events), Vestas HRBP Assistant (onboarding optimization across 3 locations, HR newsletter + award-winning well-being/engagement = internal communications + recognition programs), InGenius cross-functional process + comms + 70+ staff guides/workflows + company-wide sessions (maps to internal-comms content + new-hire enablement), NextGen employee-experience projects, ODN HR leave-data analysis. ADP appears in her canonical skills (HRIS: ADP) — exact JD-named tool match. MS Office (Word/PowerPoint) JD-named. Honest gap: she hasn't owned a US plant's HRIS data-entry as primary duty (her HRIS use is user-level), but the broad coordinator scope (onboarding, internal comms, recognition, general HR support) is squarely her work.
scope_verdict: GO. Primary function is broad HR-coordination people-support: 'providing general HR administrative support, coordination and facilitation of new hire orientation, maintenance of employee files, and coordination of employee communication and HR-related programs and events.' Duties span HRIS-maintenance (ADP) + new-hire orientation + internal-communications content creation (intranet/newsletters/toolbox talks) + employee recognition programs + commuter program. HRIS data-entry is ONE component, NOT the dominant duty — it is balanced against internal-comms, onboarding, recognition, and general employee support. This is the 'clerical-but-broad-people-support = GO' case (at-level for Jamie), not a narrow payroll/benefits-admin/ER-casework/recruiting-primary/leave-admin/HRIS-data-entry hard-stop.
notes: Role-type = HR/HRBP per content_library Tailoring Playbook.
